[Become an Ambassador](https://opencollective.com/solidus). 49: 50: [![Super Good Software](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/solidusio/brand/master/partners/SuperGood/logo-dark-light.png)](https://supergood.software/) 51: 52: --- 53: 54: ## Summary 55: 56: Solidus is a complete open source e-commerce solution built with Ruby on Rails. 57: It is a fork of [Spree](https://spreecommerce.org). 58: 59: See the [Solidus class documentation](http://docs.solidus.io) and the [Solidus 60: Guides](https://guides.solidus.io) for information about the functionality that 61: Solidus provides. 62: 63: Solidus consists of several gems. When you require the `solidus` gem in your 64: `Gemfile`, Bundler will install all of the following gems: 65: 66: - [`solidus_api`](https://github.com/solidusio/solidus/tree/master/api) (RESTful API) 67: - [`solidus_backend`](https://github.com/solidusio/solidus/tree/master/backend) (Admin area) 68: - [`solidus_core`](https://github.com/solidusio/solidus/tree/master/core) (Essential models, mailers, and classes) 69: - [`solidus_sample`](https://github.com/solidusio/solidus/tree/master/sample) (Sample data) 70: 71: All of the gems are designed to work together to provide a fully functional 72: e-commerce platform. However, you may only want to use the 73: [`solidus_core`](https://github.com/solidusio/solidus/tree/master/core) gem 74: combine it with your own custom frontend, admin interface, and API. 75: 76: ## Demo 77: 78: You can try the live Solidus demo [here.](http://demo.solidus.io/) The admin section can be accessed [here.](http://demo.solidus.io/admin) 79: 80: ## Getting started 81: 82: Begin by making sure you have 83: [Imagemagick](http://imagemagick.org/script/download.php) installed, which is 84: required for Paperclip. (You can install it using [Homebrew](https://brew.sh) if 85: you're on a Mac.) 86: 87: To add Solidus, begin with a newly created Rails application with its database. 88: 89: ```bash 90: rails new my_store 91: ``` 92: 93: > [!CAUTION] 94: > Due to [a bug in `sprockets-rails`](https://github.com/rails/sprockets-rails/pull/546) we need to manually add the sprockets manifest into the generated rails app **before** running any rails commands inside the rails app folder. 95: 96: ```bash 97: mkdir -p my_store/app/assets/config 98: cat < my_store/app/assets/config/manifest.js 99: //= link_tree ../images 100: //= link_directory ../javascripts .js 101: //= link_directory ../stylesheets .css 102: MANIFEST 103: ``` 104: 105: ### Installing Solidus 106: 107: In your application's root folder run: 108: 109: ```bash 110: bundle add solidus 111: bin/rails g solidus:install 112: ``` 113: 114: > [!NOTE] 115: > Please make sure to generate the sprockets manifest before running the `solidus:install` generator. 116: 117: And follow the prompt's instructions. 118: ### Accessing Solidus Store 119: 120: Start the Rails server with the command: 121: 122: ```bash 123: bin/rails s 124: ``` 125: 126: The storefront will be accessible at 127: [http://localhost:3000/](http://localhost:3000/) and the admin can be found at 128: [http://localhost:3000/admin/](http://localhost:3000/admin/). 129: 130: For information on how to customize your store, check out the [customization guides](https://guides.solidus.io/customization/customizing-your-storefront). 131: 132: ### Default Username/Password 133: 134: As part of running the above installation steps, you will be asked to set an admin email/password combination. The default values are `admin@example.com` and `test123`, respectively. 135: 136: ### Questions? 137: 138: The best way to ask questions is to [join the Solidus Slack](http://slack.solidus.io/) and join the [#support channel](https://solidusio.slack.com/messages/support/details/). 139: 140: ## Installation options 141: 142: Instead of a stable build, if you want to use the bleeding edge version of 143: Solidus, use this line: 144: 145: ```ruby 146: gem 'solidus', github: 'solidusio/solidus' 147: ``` 148: 149: **Note: The master branch is not guaranteed to ever be in a fully functioning 150: state. It is too risky to use this branch in production.** 151: 152: By default, the installation generator (`solidus:install`) will run 153: migrations as well as adding seed and sample data. This can be disabled using 154: 155: ```bash 156: bin/rails g solidus:install --migrate=false --sample=false --seed=false 157: ``` 158: 159: You can always perform any of these steps later by using these commands. 160: 161: ```bash 162: bin/rails railties:install:migrations 163: bin/rails db:migrate 164: bin/rails db:seed 165: bin/rails spree_sample:load 166: ``` 167: 168: There are also options and rake tasks provided by 169: [solidus\_auth\_devise](https://github.com/solidusio/solidus_auth_devise). 170: 171: ## Performance 172: 173: You may notice that your Solidus store runs slowly in development mode. This 174: can be because in development each CSS and JavaScript is loaded as a separate 175: include. This can be disabled by adding the following to 176: `config/environments/development.rb`. 177: 178: ```ruby 179: config.assets.debug = false 180: ``` 181: 182: ### Turbolinks 183: 184: To gain some extra speed you may enable Turbolinks inside of Solidus admin. 185: 186: Add `gem 'turbolinks', '~> 5.0.0'` into your `Gemfile` (if not already present) 187: and change `vendor/assets/javascripts/spree/backend/all.js` as follows: 188: 189: ```js 190: //= require turbolinks 191: // 192: // ... current file content 193: // 194: //= require spree/backend/turbolinks-integration.js 195: ``` 196: 197: **CAUTION** Please be aware that Turbolinks can break extensions 198: and/or customizations to the Solidus admin. Use at your own risk. 199: 200: ## Developing Solidus 201: 202: * Clone the Git repo 203: 204: ```bash 205: git clone git://github.com/solidusio/solidus.git 206: cd solidus 207: ``` 208: 209: ### Without Docker 210: 211: * Install the gem dependencies 212: 213: ```bash 214: bin/setup 215: ``` 216: 217: _Note_: If you're using PostgreSQL, MySQL, or MariaDB, you'll need to install those gems through the DB environment variable. 218: 219: ```bash 220: # PostgreSQL 221: export DB=postgresql 222: bin/setup 223: 224: # MySQL or MariaDB (mysql2 adapter) 225: export DB=mysql 226: bin/setup 227: ``` 228: 229: ### With Docker 230: 231: ```bash 232: docker-compose up -d 233: ``` 234: 235: Wait for all the gems to be installed (progress can be checked through `docker-compose logs -f app`). 236: 237: You can provide the ruby version you want your image to use: 238: 239: ```bash 240: docker-compose build --build-arg RUBY_VERSION=3.0 app 241: docker-compose up -d 242: ``` 243: 244: The rails version can be customized at runtime through the `RAILS_VERSION` environment variable: 245: 246: ```bash 247: RAILS_VERSION='~> 5.0' docker-compose up -d 248: ``` 249: 250: Running tests: 251: 252: ```bash 253: # sqlite 254: docker-compose exec app bin/rspec 255: # postgres 256: docker-compose exec app env DB=postgres bin/rspec 257: # mysql 258: docker-compose exec app env DB=mysql bin/rspec 259: ``` 260: 261: Accessing the databases: 262: 263: ```bash 264: # sqlite 265: docker-compose exec app sqlite3 /path/to/db 266: # postgres 267: docker-compose exec app env PGPASSWORD=password psql -U root -h postgres 268: # mysql 269: docker-compose exec app mysql -u root -h mysql -ppassword 270: ``` 271: 272: In order to be able to access the [sandbox application](#sandbox), just make 273: sure to provide the appropriate `--binding` option to `rails server`. By 274: default, port `3000` is exposed, but you can change it through `SANDBOX_PORT` 275: environment variable: 276: 277: ```bash 278: SANDBOX_PORT=4000 docker-compose up -d 279: docker-compose exec app bin/sandbox 280: docker-compose exec app bin/rails server --binding 0.0.0.0 --port 4000 281: ``` 282: 283: ### Sandbox 284: 285: Solidus is meant to be run within the context of Rails application. You can 286: easily create a sandbox application inside of your cloned source directory for 287: testing purposes. 288: 289: This sandbox includes solidus\_auth\_devise and generates with seed and sample 290: data already loaded. 291: 292: * Create the sandbox application 293: 294: ```bash 295: bin/sandbox 296: ``` 297: 298: You can create a sandbox with PostgreSQL, MySQL, or MariaDB by setting the DB environment variable. 299: 300: ```bash 301: # PostgreSQL 302: export DB=postgresql 303: bin/sandbox 304: 305: # MySQL or MariaDB (mysql2 adapter) 306: export DB=mysql 307: bin/sandbox 308: ``` 309: 310: Depending on your local environment, it may be necessary for you to set environment variables for your RDBMS, namely: 311: - `DB_HOST` 312: - `DB_USER` 313: - `DB_PASSWORD` 314: 315: If you need to create a Rails 5.2 application for your sandbox, for example 316: if you are still using Ruby 2.4 which is not supported by Rails 6, you can 317: use the `RAILS_VERSION` environment variable. 318: 319: ```bash 320: export RAILS_VERSION='~> 5.2.0' 321: bin/setup 322: bin/sandbox 323: ``` 324: 325: * You can start the Rails server and other services from either the Solidus folder or the 326: sandbox one by running the command: 327: 328: ```bash 329: bin/dev 330: ``` 331: 332: Please note: if you run `bin/rails server` or similar commands, only the Rails server will 333: start. This might cause the error `couldn't find file 'solidus_admin/tailwind.css'` when you 334: try to load admin pages. 335: 336: ### Tests 337: 338: Solidus uses [RSpec](http://rspec.info) for tests. Refer to its documentation for 339: more information about the testing library. 340: 341: #### CircleCI 342: 343: We use CircleCI to run the tests for Solidus as well as all incoming pull 344: requests. All pull requests must pass to be merged. 345: 346: You can see the build statuses at 347: [https://circleci.com/gh/solidusio/solidus](https://circleci.com/gh/solidusio/solidus). 348: 349: #### Run all tests 350: 351: [ChromeDriver](https://chromedriver.chromium.org/downloads) is required to run 352: the backend test suites. 353: 354: To execute all of the test specs, run the `bin/build` script at the root of the Solidus project: 355: 356: ```bash 357: createuser --superuser --echo postgres # only the first time 358: bin/build 359: ``` 360: 361: The `bin/build` script runs using PostgreSQL by default, but it can be overridden by setting the DB environment variable to `DB=sqlite` or `DB=mysql` (MariaDB uses `DB=mysql`). For example: 362: 363: ```bash 364: env DB=mysql bin/build 365: ``` 366: 367: If the command fails with MySQL or MariaDB related errors you can try creating a user with this command: 368: 369: ```bash 370: # Creates a user with the same name as the current user and no restrictions. 371: mysql --user="root" --execute="CREATE USER '$USER'@'localhost'; GRANT ALL PRIVILEGES ON * . * TO '$USER'@'localhost';" 372: ``` 373: 374: #### Run an individual test suite 375: 376: Each gem contains its own series of tests. To run the tests for the core project: 377: 378: ```bash 379: cd core 380: bundle exec rspec 381: ``` 382: 383: By default, `rspec` runs the tests for SQLite 3. If you would like to run specs 384: against another database you may specify the database in the command: 385: 386: ```bash 387: env DB=postgresql bundle exec rspec 388: ``` 389: 390: #### Code coverage reports 391: 392: If you want to run the [SimpleCov](https://github.com/colszowka/simplecov) code 393: coverage report: 394: 395: ```bash 396: COVERAGE=true bundle exec rspec 397: ``` 398: 399: ### Extensions 400: 401: In addition to core functionality provided in Solidus, there are a number of 402: ways to add features to your store that are not (or not yet) part of the core 403: project. 404: 405: A list can be found at [extensions.solidus.io](http://extensions.solidus.io/). 406: 407: If you want to write an extension for Solidus, you can use the 408: [solidus_dev_support](https://github.com/solidusio/solidus_dev_support.git) gem. 409: 410: ## Contributing 411: 412: Solidus is an open source project and we encourage contributions. 585:     def add_store_credit_payments
586:       return if user.nil?
587:       return if payments.store_credits.checkout.empty? && user.available_store_credit_total(currency:).zero?
588: 
589:       payments.store_credits.checkout.each(&:invalidate!)
590: 
591:       # this can happen when multiple payments are present, auto_capture is
592:       # turned off, and one of the payments fails when the user tries to
593:       # complete the order, which sends the order back to the 'payment' state.
594:       authorized_total = payments.pending.sum(:amount)
595: 
596:       remaining_total = outstanding_balance - authorized_total
597: 
598:       matching_store_credits = user.store_credits.where(currency:)
599: 
600:       if matching_store_credits.any?
601:         payment_method = Spree::PaymentMethod::StoreCredit.first
602:         sorter = Spree::Config.store_credit_prioritizer_class.new(matching_store_credits, self)
603: 
604:         sorter.call.each do |credit|
605:           break if remaining_total.zero?
606:           next if credit.amount_remaining.zero?
607: 
608:           amount_to_take = [credit.amount_remaining, remaining_total].min
609:           payments.create!(source: credit,
610:             payment_method:,
611:             amount: amount_to_take,
612:             state: "checkout",
613:             response_code: credit.generate_authorization_code)
614:           remaining_total -= amount_to_take
615:         end
616:       end
617: 
618:       other_payments = payments.checkout.not_store_credits
619:       if remaining_total.zero?
620:         other_payments.each(&:invalidate!)
621:       elsif other_payments.size == 1
622:         other_payments.first.update!(amount: remaining_total)
623:       end
624: 
625:       payments.reset
626: 
627:       if payments.where(state: %w[checkout pending completed]).sum(:amount) != total
628:         errors.add(:base, I18n.t("spree.store_credit.errors.unable_to_fund")) && (return false)
629:       end
630:     end

756:     # Finalizes an in progress order after checkout is complete.
757:     # Called after transition to complete state when payments will have been processed
758:     def finalize
759:       # lock all adjustments (coupon promotions, etc.)
760:       all_adjustments.each(&:finalize!)
761: 
762:       # update payment and shipment(s) states, and save
763:       recalculator.recalculate_payment_state
764:       shipments.each do |shipment|
765:         shipment.update_state
766:         shipment.finalize!
767:       end
768: 
769:       recalculator.recalculate_shipment_state
770:       save!
771: 
772:       touch :completed_at
773:

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Spree
4:   # Spree::OrderMerger is responsible for taking two orders and merging them
5:   # together by adding the line items from additional orders to the order
6:   # that the OrderMerger is initialized with.
7:   #
8:   # Orders that are merged should be incomplete orders which should belong to
9:   # the same user. They should also be in the same currency.
10:   class OrderMerger
11:     # @!attribute order
12:     #   @api public
13:     #   @return [Spree::Order] The order which items wll be merged into.
14:     attr_accessor :order
15: 
16:     # Create the OrderMerger
17:     #
18:     # @api public
19:     # @param [Spree::Order] order The order which line items will be merged
20:     # into.
21:     def initialize(order)
22:       @order = order
23:     end
24: 
25:     # Merge a second order in to the order the OrderMerger was initialized with
26:     #
27:     # The line items from `other_order` will be merged in to the `order` for
28:     # this OrderMerger object. If the line items are for the same variant, it
29:     # will add the quantity of the incoming line item to the existing line item.
30:     # Otherwise, it will assign the line item to the new order.
31:     #
32:     # After the orders have been merged the `other_order` will be destroyed.
33:     #
34:     # @example
35:     #   initial_order = Spree::Order.find(1)
36:     #   order_to_merge = Spree::Order.find(2)
37:     #   merger = Spree::OrderMerger.new(initial_order)
38:     #   merger.merge!(order_to_merge)
39:     #   # order_to_merge is destroyed, initial order now contains the line items
40:     #   # of order_to_merge
41:     #
42:     # @api public
43:     # @param [Spree::Order] other_order An order which will be merged in to the
44:     # order the OrderMerger was initialized with.
45:     # @param [Spree::User] user Associate the order the user specified. If not
46:     # specified, the order user association will not be changed.
47:     # @return [void]
48:     def merge!(other_order, user = nil)
49:       if other_order.currency == order.currency
50:         other_order.line_items.each do |other_order_line_item|
51:           current_line_item = find_matching_line_item(other_order_line_item)
52:           handle_merge(current_line_item, other_order_line_item)
53:         end
54:       end
55: 
56:       set_user(user)
57:       if order.valid?
58:         persist_merge
59: 
60:         # So that the destroy doesn't take out line items which may have been re-assigned
61:         other_order.line_items.reload
62:         other_order.destroy
63:       end
64:     end
65: 
66:     private
67: 
68:     # Retreive a matching line item from the existing order
69:     #
70:     # It will compare line items based on variants, and all line item
71:     # comparison hooks on the order.
72:     #
73:     # @api private
74:     # @param [Spree::LineItem] other_order_line_item The line item from
75:     # `other_order` we are attempting to merge in.
76:     # @return [Spree::LineItem] A matching line item from the order. nil if none
77:     # exist.
78:     def find_matching_line_item(other_order_line_item)
79:       order.line_items.detect do |my_li|
80:         my_li.variant == other_order_line_item.variant &&
81:           Spree::Config.line_item_comparison_hooks.all? do |hook|
82:             order.send(hook, my_li, other_order_line_item.serializable_hash)
83:           end
84:       end
85:     end
86: 
87:     # Associate the user with the order
88:     #
89:     # @api private
90:     # @param [Spree::User] user The user to associate with the order. If nil
91:     # the order user association will remain the same. If the order is already
92:     # associated with a user, it will not be changed.
93:     # @return [void]
94:     def set_user(user)
95:       order.associate_user!(user) if !order.user && user
96:     end
97: 
98:     # Merge the `other_order_line_item` into `current_line_item`
99:     #
100:     # If `current_line_item` is nil, the `other_order_line_item` will be
101:     # re-assigned to the `order`.
102:     #
103:     # If the merged line item can not be saved, an error will be added to
104:     # `order`.
105:     #
106:     # @api private
107:     # @param [Spree::LineItem] current_line_item The line item to be merged
108:     # into. If nil, the `other_order_line_item` will be re-assigned.
109:     # @param [Spree::LineItem] other_order_line_item The line item to merge in.
110:     # @return [void]
111:     def handle_merge(current_line_item, other_order_line_item)
112:       if current_line_item
113:         current_line_item.quantity += other_order_line_item.quantity
114:         handle_error(current_line_item) unless current_line_item.save
115:       else
116:         new_line_item = order.line_items.build(other_order_line_item.attributes.except("id"))
117:         handle_error(new_line_item) unless new_line_item.save
118:       end
119:     end
120: 
121:     # Handle an error from saving the `line_item`
122:     #
123:     # This adds errors from the line item to the `errors[:base]` of the order.
124:     #
125:     # @api private
126:     # @param [Spree::LineItem] line_item The line item which could not be saved
127:     # @return [void]
128:     def handle_error(line_item)
129:       order.errors.add(:base, line_item.errors.full_messages)
130:     end
131: 
132:     # Save the order totals after merge
133:     #
134:     # It triggers the order recalculator to ensure that item counts and totals are
135:     # up to date.
136:     #
137:     # @api private
138:     # @return [void]
139:     def persist_merge
140:       order.recalculate
141:     end
142:   end
143: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true

3: module Spree
4:   # Relatively simple class used to apply a {Spree::Tax::OrderTax} to a
5:   # {Spree::Order}.
6:   #
7:   # This class will create or update adjustments on the taxed items and remove
8:   # any now inapplicable tax adjustments from the order.
9:   class OrderTaxation
10:     # Create a new order taxation.
11:     #
12:     # @param [Spree::Order] order the order to apply taxes to
13:     # @return [Spree::OrderTaxation] a {Spree::OrderTaxation} object
14:     def initialize(order)
15:       @order = order
16:     end
17: 
18:     # Apply taxes to the order.
19:     #
20:     # This method will create or update adjustments on the order and all line
21:     # items and shipments in the order to reflect the appropriate taxes passed
22:     # in. It will also remove any now inapplicable tax adjustments.
23:     #
24:     # @param [Spree::Tax::OrderTax] taxes the taxes to apply to the order
25:     # @return [void]
26:     def apply(taxes)
27:       update_adjustments(@order, taxes.order_taxes) if taxes.order_taxes
28: 
29:       @order.line_items.each do |item|
30:         taxed_items = taxes.line_item_taxes.select { |element| element.item_id == item.id }
31:         item.tax_category_id = item.variant_tax_category_id
32:         update_adjustments(item, taxed_items)
33:       end
34: 
35:       @order.shipments.each do |item|
36:         taxed_items = taxes.shipment_taxes.select { |element| element.item_id == item.id }
37:         update_adjustments(item, taxed_items)
38:       end
39:     end
40: 
41:     private
42: 
43:     # Walk through the taxes for an item and update adjustments for it. Once
44:     # all of the taxes have been added as adjustments, remove any old tax
45:     # adjustments that weren't touched.
46:     #
47:     # @private
48:     # @param [#adjustments] item a {Spree::LineItem} or {Spree::Shipment}
49:     # @param [Array<Spree::Tax::ItemTax>] taxed_items a list of calculated taxes for an item
50:     # @return [void]
51:     def update_adjustments(item, taxed_items)
52:       tax_adjustments = item.adjustments.select(&:tax?)
53: 
54:       active_adjustments = taxed_items.map do |tax_item|
55:         update_adjustment(item, tax_item)
56:       end
57: 
58:       # Remove any tax adjustments tied to rates which no longer match.
59:       unmatched_adjustments = tax_adjustments - active_adjustments
60:       unmatched_adjustments.each(&:mark_for_destruction)
61:     end
62: 
63:     # Update or create a new tax adjustment on an item.
64:     #
65:     # @private
66:     # @param [#adjustments] item a {Spree::LineItem} or {Spree::Shipment}
67:     # @param [Spree::Tax::ItemTax] tax_item calculated taxes for an item
68:     # @return [Spree::Adjustment] the created or updated tax adjustment
69:     def update_adjustment(item, tax_item)
70:       tax_adjustment = item.adjustments.detect do |adjustment|
71:         adjustment.source == tax_item.tax_rate
72:       end
73: 
74:       tax_adjustment ||= item.adjustments.new(
75:         source: tax_item.tax_rate,
76:         order_id: item.is_a?(Spree::Order) ? item.id : item.order_id,
77:         label: tax_item.label,
78:         included: tax_item.included_in_price
79:       )
80: 
81:       tax_adjustment.amount = tax_item.amount
82:       tax_adjustment
83:     end
84:   end
85: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module Spree
4:   class OrderInventory
5:     attr_accessor :order, :line_item, :variant
6: 
7:     def initialize(order, line_item)
8:       @order = order
9:       @line_item = line_item
10:       @variant = line_item.variant
11:     end
12: 
13:     # Only verify inventory for completed orders (as orders in frontend checkout
14:     # have inventory assigned via +order.create_proposed_shipment+) or when
15:     # shipment is explicitly passed
16:     #
17:     # In case shipment is passed the stock location should only unstock or
18:     # restock items if the order is completed. That is so because stock items
19:     # are always unstocked when the order is completed through +shipment.finalize+
20:     def verify(shipment = nil)
21:       if order.completed? || shipment.present?
22: 
23:         existing_quantity = inventory_units.count
24:         desired_quantity = line_item.quantity - existing_quantity
25:         if desired_quantity > 0
26:           shipment ||= determine_target_shipment(desired_quantity)
27:           if shipment
28:             add_to_shipment(shipment, desired_quantity)
29:           else
30:             order.create_shipments_for_line_item(line_item).each do |new_shipment|
31:               new_shipment.finalize!
32:             end
33:           end
34:         elsif desired_quantity < 0
35:           remove(-desired_quantity, shipment)
36:         end
37:       end
38:     end
39: 
40:     def inventory_units
41:       line_item.inventory_units
42:     end
43: 
44:     private
45: 
46:     def remove(quantity, shipment = nil)
47:       if shipment.present?
48:         remove_from_shipment(shipment, quantity)
49:       else
50:         remove_from_any_shipment(quantity)
51:       end
52:     end
53: 
54:     # Returns either one of the shipment:
55:     #
56:     # first unshipped that already includes this variant
57:     # first unshipped that's leaving from a stock_location that stocks this variant, with availability check
58:     # first unshipped that's leaving from a stock_location that stocks this variant, without availability check
59:     def determine_target_shipment(quantity)
60:       potential_shipments = order.shipments.order(:created_at, :id).select(&:ready_or_pending?)
61: 
62:       potential_shipments.detect do |shipment|
63:         shipment.include?(variant)
64:       end || begin
65:         potential_shipments.detect do |shipment|
66:           Spree::Config.stock.quantifier_class.new(variant, shipment.stock_location).can_supply?(quantity)
67:         end
68:       end || potential_shipments.detect do |shipment|
69:         variant.stock_location_ids.include?(shipment.stock_location_id)
70:       end
71:     end
72: 
73:     def add_to_shipment(shipment, quantity)
74:       pending_units = []
75:       if variant.should_track_inventory?
76:         on_hand, back_order = shipment.stock_location.fill_status(variant, quantity)
77: 
78:         on_hand.times { pending_units << shipment.set_up_inventory("on_hand", variant, order, line_item) }
79:         back_order.times { pending_units << shipment.set_up_inventory("backordered", variant, order, line_item) }
80:       else
81:         quantity.times { pending_units << shipment.set_up_inventory("on_hand", variant, order, line_item) }
82:       end
83: 
84:       # adding to this shipment, and removing from stock_location
85:       if order.completed?
86:         Spree::Stock::InventoryUnitsFinalizer.new(pending_units).run!
87:       end
88: 
89:       quantity
90:     end
91: 
92:     def remove_from_any_shipment(quantity)
93:       order.shipments.each do |shipment|
94:         break if quantity == 0
95:         quantity -= remove_from_shipment(shipment, quantity)
96:       end
97:     end
98: 
99:     def remove_from_shipment(shipment, quantity)
100:       return 0 if quantity == 0 || shipment.shipped?
101: 
102:       shipment_units = shipment.inventory_units_for_item(line_item, variant).reject do |variant_unit|
103:         # TODO: exclude all 'shipped' states
104:         variant_unit.state == "shipped"
105:       end.sort_by(&:state)
106: 
107:       removed_quantity = 0
108: 
109:       shipment_units.each do |inventory_unit|
110:         break if removed_quantity == quantity
111:         inventory_unit.destroy
112:         removed_quantity += 1
113:       end
114: 
115:       if shipment.inventory_units.count.zero?
116:         order.shipments.destroy(shipment)
117:       end
118: 
119:       # removing this from shipment, and adding to stock_location
120:       if order.completed?
121:         shipment.stock_location.restock variant, removed_quantity, shipment
122:       end
123: 
124:       removed_quantity
125:     end
126:   end
127: end

5:     class LockFailed < StandardError; end
6: 
7:     belongs_to :order, class_name: "Spree::Order", optional: true
8: 
9:     scope :expired, -> { where(arel_table[:created_at].lteq(Spree::Config[:order_mutex_max_age].seconds.ago)) }
10: 
11:     class << self
12:       # Obtain a lock on an order, execute the supplied block and then release the lock.
13:       # Raise a LockFailed exception immediately if we cannot obtain the lock.
14:       # We raise instead of blocking to avoid tying up multiple server processes waiting for the lock.
15:       def with_lock!(order)
16:         raise ArgumentError, "order must be supplied" if order.nil?
17: 
18:         # limit the maximum lock time just in case a lock is somehow left in place accidentally
19:         expired.where(order:).delete_all
20: 
21:         begin
22:           order_mutex = create!(order:)
23:         rescue ActiveRecord::RecordNotUnique
24:           error = LockFailed.new("Could not obtain lock on order #{order.id}")
25:           logger.error error.inspect
26:           raise error
27:         end
28: 
29:         yield
30:       ensure
31:         order_mutex&.destroy
32:       end
33:     end
34:   end
